text.title: Definition of Done Template Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Definition of Done Template application MUST enforce, plus a skip-this-methodology fallback. These are HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: DoD MUST be defined per artefact type (story, spike, bugfix, refactor, infra change). A single global DoD is rejected.
rule.rationale: Each artefact type has different ship criteria (a spike is done when the question is answered, not when tests are green); a global DoD over-includes or under-includes for half the work.

rule.statement: Every DoD item MUST be a binary yes/no check with an observable artefact (commit SHA, test run id, doc URL). Subjective items ('code is clean') are rejected.
rule.rationale: Subjective items collapse to whichever reviewer is in the room; binary + observable items are auditable and consistent.

rule.statement: Every DoD record MUST carry a named human signoff per gate (tests-by, docs-by, runbook-by, PM-by). 'Team' is rejected.
rule.rationale: Diffused accountability means none; named signoff anchors the DoD to a person who can be asked later.

rule.statement: DoD for any user-facing or business-logic change MUST include 'telemetry added' or an explicit 'no telemetry needed because X' rationale. Skipping the gate is rejected.
rule.rationale: Without explicit telemetry handling, post-ship blindness compounds; the explicit gate forces a deliberate decision either way.

rule.statement: Every shipped artefact MUST emit a DoD record stored alongside the PR or ticket and findable by SHA. Un-recorded DoD is rejected at the release gate.
rule.rationale: Verbal or chat-only DoD is unverifiable in audits and retros; the persistent record is the only artefact that survives memory.

rule.statement: If any Applies-If precondition fails OR any Skip-If condition triggers, do NOT apply this methodology. Emit a skip-record naming the failed precondition / triggered skip, and route the task to the correct methodology (or none).
rule.rationale: Methodology hygiene: mis-application produces worse artefacts than no methodology because downstream consumers trust the schema; the skip-record makes non-application traceable.
